hops.context: The GAP8 demonstrates the highest end-to-end latency across all models - averaging 17× slower than the MAX78000
Another unexpected finding is the superior inference latency of the MAX78000 compared to the HX-WE2

question: Considering the performance comparison of different hardware platforms in terms of inference latency, which platform is classified as having the highest end-to-end latency?

options.A: HX-WE2, as it outperforms GAP8 and MAX78000 in terms of end-to-end latency.
options.B: HX-WE2, as it is outperformed by both MAX78000 and GAP8 in terms of inference latency.
options.C: GAP8, as it is mentioned to be 17 times slower than the MAX78000 in terms of end-to-end latency.
options.D: MAX78000, as it has superior inference latency compared to the HX-WE2 but slower than the GAP8.

explanation: The correct answer is A because the context clearly states that GAP8 has the highest end-to-end latency, averaging 17 times slower than the MAX78000, indicating it performs worse in latency compared to the other platforms mentioned.
context: The GAP8 demonstrates the highest end-to-end latency across all models - averaging 17× slower than the MAX78000 | Another unexpected finding is the superior inference latency of the MAX78000 compared to the HX-WE2 | and beating the HX-WE2
